scenarios.title: AI Agent Framework Development
scenarios.when_to_read: Tasks that build, test, debug, or maintain applications using Pydantic AI agents, tools, models, outputs, MCP, CLI, evals, graph workflows, or related packages.
scenarios.role: Provides focused Pydantic AI ecosystem routing and self-contained references for application and framework tasks.
scenarios.read_when: User mentions Pydantic AI, pydantic_ai, pydantic-graph, pydantic-evals, clai, Agent, RunContext, Tool, TestModel, FunctionToolset, ModelRetry, MCP, GraphBuilder, Dataset, Case, pai, or clai web.
scenarios.best_for: Agent construction, typed deps, streaming, tools/toolsets, structured outputs, provider configuration, optional extras, MCP/capabilities/hooks, evals, graph workflows, CLI apps, and deterministic tests.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task is about the standalone Pydantic validation library without Pydantic AI, an unrelated AI framework, or generic Python code with no Pydantic AI APIs.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `pydantic-ai` over generic agent-framework guidance when the code, docs, errors, or dependencies use Pydantic AI-specific APIs or packages; route within the skill by the task family table in the root SKILL.md.

scenarios.title: Python Open Source Repository Maintenance
scenarios.when_to_read: Tasks that edit, review, test, document, or maintain the Pydantic AI monorepo itself.
scenarios.role: Captures Pydantic AI repository layout, contribution philosophy, targeted validation, cassette workflows, docs examples, and generated-skill refresh expectations.
scenarios.read_when: User mentions editing this checkout, AGENTS.md, agent_docs, uv workspace, pyright, ruff, pytest cassettes, VCR, inline snapshots, docs examples, PR review, or Pydantic AI maintainer workflows.
scenarios.best_for: Choosing narrow validation commands, recording or replaying provider cassettes, updating docs/examples/tests, reviewing public API changes, and refreshing generated repo skills after code drift.
scenarios.avoid_when: The user only wants to use Pydantic AI as an installed dependency and is not editing the repository.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Use the repo-development sub-skill for maintainer tasks, and use the user-facing sub-skills when the task is about application code that consumes Pydantic AI.
